info.title: SignRequest API
info.description: SignRequest is a digital signature solution. This API allows you to create and manage document signing requests.

get.summary: Retrieve a list of API tokens

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

x-code-samples.source:
<?php

$config = SignRequest\Configuration::getDefaultConfiguration();

$apiInstance = new SignRequest\Api\ApiTokensApi(new GuzzleHttp\Client(), $config);
$page = 1;
$limit = 20;

$result = $apiInstance->apiTokensList($page, $limit);

?>

import signrequest_client

api_instance = signrequest_client.ApiTokensApi()

api_response = api_instance.api_tokens_list(page=1, limit=20)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: event_type
parameters.description: `login_failed`: Login failed, `login_successful`: Login successful, `password_reset_request_sent`: Password reset request sent, `password_reset_request_error`: Password reset request error

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: `success`: Success, `error`: Error

parameters.name: timestamp__date__lt
parameters.description: Date format YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.name: timestamp__date__lte
parameters.description: Date format YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.name: timestamp__date__gt
parameters.description: Date format YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.name: timestamp__date__gte
parameters.description: Date format YYYY-MM-DD

get.summary: Retrieve a list of Document Attachments

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

x-code-samples.source:
<?php

$config = SignRequest\Configuration::getDefaultConfiguration();

$apiInstance = new SignRequest\Api\DocumentAttachmentsApi(new GuzzleHttp\Client(), $config);

$document__uuid = "document__uuid_example";
$document__external_id = "document__external_id_example";
$created = "created_example";
$page = 1;
$limit = 20;

$result = $apiInstance->documentAttachmentsList($document__uuid, $document__external_id, $created, $page, $limit);

?>

import signrequest_client

api_instance = signrequest_client.DocumentAttachmentsApi()

api_instance.document_attachments_list(page=1, limit=20)

<?php

$config = SignRequest\Configuration::getDefaultConfiguration();

$apiInstance = new SignRequest\Api\DocumentAttachmentsApi(new GuzzleHttp\Client(), $config);
$data = new \SignRequest\Model\DocumentAttachment();
$data->setFileFromUrl('https://example.com/file.pdf');
$data->setDocument('https://example.com/doc');

$result = $apiInstance->documentAttachmentsCreate($data);

?>

import signrequest_client

api_instance = signrequest_client.DocumentAttachmentsApi()
data = signrequest_client.DocumentAttachment(
    file_from_url="https://example.com/file.pdf", document="https://example.com/doc"
)

api_response = api_instance.document_attachments_create(data)

api_instance = SignRequestClient::DocumentAttachmentsApi.new

data.file_from_url = 'https://example.com/file.pdf'
data.document = 'https://example.com/doc'

result = api_instance.document_attachments_create(data)

$apiInstance = new SignRequest\Api\DocumentAttachmentsApi(new GuzzleHttp\Client(), $config);

$result = $apiInstance->documentAttachmentsRead($uuid);

?>

api_instance = SignRequestClient::DocumentAttachmentsApi.new
uuid = 'uuid_example'

result = api_instance.document_attachments_read(uuid)

get.description:
Search interface for fast (autocomplete) searching of documents.

This can be useful to have your users search for a document in your interface.

Document names are tokenized on whitespace, hyphens and underscores to also match partial document names.

*Normal search:*

- ?**q**={{query}}

*Autocomplete search:*

- ?**autocomplete**={{partial query}}

*Search in document name:*

- ?**name**={{query}}

*Available (extra) filters:*

- ?**subdomain**={{ team_subdomain }} or use this endpoint with team_subdomain.signrequest.com
(when not provided only personal documents are shown)
- ?**signer_emails**={{ signer@email.com }} (will filter documents that an email needed to sign/approve)
- ?**status**={{ si }}
- ?**who**={{ mo }}

To include multiple values for a filter field separate the values with a pipe (|).
For example to only search for completed documents use **status=se|vi** (sent and viewed).

*Pagination:*

- ?**page**={{ page_number: default 1 }}
- ?**limit**={{ limit results: default 10, max 100 }}

*Format:*

By default json is returned, to export data as csv or xls use the format parameter.

- ?**format**=csv
- ?**format**=xls

For csv and xls the data can also be exported with each signer on a separate row. In this mode also the signer
inputs that have an *external_id* specified on a tag will be exported. All external_id's found will be exported as
columns. To use this mode add the **signer_data** parameter.

- ?**format**=csv&**signer_data**=1
- ?**format**=xls&**signer_data**=1

Note that all documents are only ordered by **created** (newest first) when **q**, **autocomplete** or **name** are
not used, else they are ordered by the strenght of the match.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Normal search query

parameters.name: autocomplete
parameters.description: Partial search query

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Document name

parameters.name: signer_emails
parameters.description: Email needed to sign/approve

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: `co`: converting, `ne`: new, `se`: sent, `vi`: viewed, `si`: signed, `do`: downloaded, `sd`: signed and downloaded, `ca`: cancelled, `de`: declined, `ec`: error converting, `es`: error sending, `xp`: expired

parameters.name: who
parameters.description: `m`: only me, `mo`: me and others, `o`: only others

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Export format, can be `json` (default), `csv`, or `xls`

parameters.name: signer_data
parameters.description: Set to `1` to export with each signer on a separate row

x-code-samples.source:
<?php

$config = SignRequest\Configuration::getDefaultConfiguration();

$apiInstance = new SignRequest\Api\DocumentsSearchApi(new GuzzleHttp\Client(), $config);
$page = 1;
$limit = 20;
$q = "q_example";
$autocomplete = "autocomplete_example";
$name = "name_example";
$subdomain = "subdomain_example";
$signer_emails = "someone@example.com";
$status = "si";
$who = "o";
$format = "json";
$signer_data = 1;

$result = $apiInstance->documentsSearchList($page, $limit, $q, $autocomplete, $name, $subdomain, $signer_emails, $status, $who, $format, $signer_data);

?>

import signrequest_client

api_instance = signrequest_client.DocumentsSearchApi()

api_response = api_instance.documents_search_list(
    page=1,
    limit=20,
    q="q_example",
    autocomplete="autocomplete_example",
    name="name_example",
    subdomain="subdomain_example",
    signer_emails="someone@example.com",
    status="si",
    who="o",
    format="json",
    signer_data=1,
)

api_instance = SignRequestClient::DocumentsSearchApi.new

opts = {
  page: 1,
  limit: 20,
  q: 'q_example',
  autocomplete: 'autocomplete_example',
  name: 'name_example',
  subdomain: 'subdomain_example',
  signer_emails: 'someone@example.com',
  status: 'si',
  who: 'o',
  format: 'json',
  signer_data: 1
}

result = api_instance.documents_search_list(opts)

$user__last_name = "user__last_name_example";

get.summary: Retrieve a list of Team Members

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

x-code-samples.source:
<?php

$apiInstance = new SignRequest\Api\TeamMembersApi(new GuzzleHttp\Client(), $config);

$is_owner = "true";
$is_admin = "true";

$user__first_name = "user__first_name_example";
$user__last_name = "user__last_name_example";

$limit = 20;

?>

get.description:
Required fields are **name** and **subdomain** where the subdomain is globally unique.
Use **POST** to create a Team.
To update a field on a Team use **PATCH**.

To use the API on behalf of a particular team change the endpoint to:
*https://**{{ subdomain }}**.signrequest.com/api/v1/...*

To invite new team members you can use **POST**
{"email":"**email-of-member-to-invite@example.com**","is_admin":false,"is_owner":false} to:
*https://signrequest.com/api/v1/teams/**{{ subdomain }}**/invite_member/*

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

Use **POST** to create a Team.
To update a field on a Team use **PATCH**.

To use the API on behalf of a particular team change the endpoint to:

To invite new team members you can use **POST**

x-code-samples.source:
<?php

$config = SignRequest\Configuration::getDefaultConfiguration();

$apiInstance = new SignRequest\Api\TeamsApi(new GuzzleHttp\Client(), $config);
$data = new \SignRequest\Model\Team();
$data->setName('Your team name');
$data->setSubdomain('your_team');

$result = $apiInstance->teamsCreate($data);

?>

import signrequest_client

api_instance = signrequest_client.TeamsApi()

api_instance = SignRequestClient::TeamsApi.new
data = SignRequestClient::Team.new
data.name = 'Your team name'
data.subdomain = 'your_team'

result = api_instance.teams_create(data)

{"email":"**email-of-member-to-invite@example.com**","is_admin":false,"is_owner":false} to:
*https://signrequest.com/api/v1/teams/**{{ subdomain }}**/invite_member/*

x-code-samples.source:
<?php

$config = SignRequest\Configuration::getDefaultConfiguration();

$apiInstance = new SignRequest\Api\TeamsApi(new GuzzleHttp\Client(), $config);
$subdomain = "subdomain_example";
$data = new \SignRequest\Model\InviteMember();
$data->setEmail('invited_user@example.com');

$result = $apiInstance->teamsInviteMember($subdomain, $data);

?>

import signrequest_client

api_instance = signrequest_client.TeamsApi()
subdomain = "subdomain_example"
data = signrequest_client.InviteMember(email="invited_user@example.com")

api_response = api_instance.teams_invite_member(subdomain, data)

api_instance = SignRequestClient::TeamsApi.new
subdomain = 'subdomain_example'
data = SignRequestClient::InviteMember.new
data.email = 'invited_user@example.com'

result = api_instance.teams_invite_member(subdomain, data)

file.title: File
file.description: Temporary URL to document attachment, expires in five minutes

file_from_content_name.title: File from content name
file_from_content_name.description: Filename, including extension. Required when using `file_from_content`.

file_from_url.title: File from url
file_from_url.description: Publicly accessible URL of document to be downloaded by SignRequest

needs_to_sign.title: Needs to sign
needs_to_sign.description: When `false` user does not need to sign, but will receive a copy of the signed document and signing log, see: [Copy only](#section/Additional-signing-methods/Copy-only)

approve_only.title: Approve only
approve_only.description: Require user to approve the document (without adding a signature), see: [Approve only](#section/Additional-signing-methods/Approve-only)

notify_only.title: Notify only
notify_only.description: Send notifications about the document and a copy of the signed document and signing log, but don't require them to take any action, see: [Notify only](#section/Additional-signing-methods/Notify-only)

in_person.title: In person
in_person.description: When used in combination with an embed url on the sender, after sender has signed, they will be redirected to the next `in_person` signer, see: [In person signing](#section/Additional-signing-methods/In-person-signing)

use_stamp_for_approve_only.title: Use stamp for approve only
use_stamp_for_approve_only.description: Place an approval stamp on a document when a signer approves a document

password.title: Password
password.description: Require the signer to enter this password before signing a document. This field is write only.

from_email.title: From email
from_email.description: Email of user sending the SignRequest (must be a validated email)

from_email_name.title: From email name
from_email_name.description: Name to be used in the `From` email header, e.g. `{from_email_name} <no-reply@signrequest.com>`

is_being_prepared.title: Is being prepared
is_being_prepared.description: Have the sender of a SignRequest prepare the document before sending the request out, see: [prepare using the web interface](#section/Preparing-a-document/Prepare-using-the-web-interface)

redirect_url.title: Redirect url
redirect_url.description: URL at which SignRequest will redirect to when a document is signed

redirect_url_declined.title: Redirect url declined
redirect_url_declined.description: URL at which SignRequest will redirect to when a document is declined

disable_text_signatures.title: Disable text signatures
disable_text_signatures.description: Disable usage of signatures generated by typing (text)

disable_emails.title: Disable emails
disable_emails.description: Disable all SignRequest status emails as well as the email that contains the signed documents

force_signature_color.title: Force signature color
force_signature_color.description: Force specific color for the signature

disable_blockchain_proof.title: Disable blockchain proof
disable_blockchain_proof.description: Disables storing timestamp proof hashes in blockchain integrations.

text_message_verification_locked.title: Text message verification locked
text_message_verification_locked.description: When true a text message verification is needed before the signer can see the document

message.title: Message
message.description: Message to include in SignRequest email, may contain the following html tags: `a`, `abbr`, `acronym`, `b`, `blockquote`, `code`, `em`, `i`, `ul`, `li`, `ol`, and `strong`

who.title: Who
who.description: `m`: only me, `mo`: me and others, `o`: only others

send_reminders.title: Send reminders
send_reminders.description: Automatically remind signers to sign a document, see: [automatic reminders](#section/Working-with-a-SignRequest/Automatic-reminders)

pdf.title: Pdf
pdf.description: Temporary URL to signing log, expires in five minutes

file_as_pdf.title: File as pdf
file_as_pdf.description: Temporary URL to original file as PDF, expires in five minutes

external_id.title: External id
external_id.description: ID used to reference document in external system

frontend_id.title: Frontend id
frontend_id.description: Shared secret used in conjunction with SignRequest-js client to grant user access to a document that's not a member of the document's team

file.title: File
file.description: Temporary URL to original file, expires in five minutes

file_from_url.title: File from url
file_from_url.description: Publicly accessible URL of document to be downloaded by SignRequest

events_callback_url.title: Events callback url
events_callback_url.description: URL at which to receive [event callbacks](#section/Events/Events-callback) for this document

file_from_content_name.title: File from content name
file_from_content_name.description: Filename, including extension. Required when using `file_from_content`.

auto_delete_days.title: Auto delete days
auto_delete_days.description: Number of days after which a finished document (signed/cancelled/declined) will be automatically deleted

auto_expire_days.title: Auto expire days
auto_expire_days.description: Number of days after which a non finished document will be automatically expired

pdf.title: Pdf
pdf.description: Temporary URL to signed document as PDF, expires in five minutes

status.title: Status
status.description: `co`: converting, `ne`: new, `se`: sent, `vi`: viewed, `si`: signed, `do`: downloaded, `sd`: signed and downloaded, `ca`: cancelled, `de`: declined, `ec`: error converting, `es`: error sending, `xp`: expired

from_email.title: From email
from_email.description: Email of user sending the SignRequest (must be a validated email)

from_email_name.title: From email name
from_email_name.description: Name to be used in the `From` email header, e.g. `{from_email_name} <no-reply@signrequest.com>`

is_being_prepared.title: Is being prepared
is_being_prepared.description: Have the sender of a SignRequest prepare the document before sending the request out, see: [prepare using the web interface](#section/Preparing-a-document/Prepare-using-the-web-interface)

force_signature_color.description: Force specific color for the signature

disable_blockchain_proof.description: Disables storing timestamp proof hashes in blockchain integrations.

api_used.description: Indicates whether document was created using the API

auto_delete_after.description: Date and time calculated using `auto_delete_days` after which a finished document (signed/cancelled/declined) will be automatically deleted

sandbox.title: Sandbox
sandbox.description: Indicates whether document was created as part of a sandbox team

auto_expire_after.title: Auto expire after
auto_expire_after.description: Date and time calculated using `auto_expire_days` after which a non finished document will be automatically expired

processing.title: Processing
processing.description: Indicates whether a change to the document is processing and the PDF may be out of date. It is recommended to wait until processing has finished before downloading the PDF. Webhooks are not sent until processing has been completed.
